claim_status: Backup-ownership readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no security review, no release-operations review, no legal review, no support review, no all-critical-scope review, no named qualified backups, no owner identity verification, no role level review, no subsystem competence review, no representative path coverage, no recent review record, no availability record, no succession evidence, no recusal review, no inactive owner replacement, no CODEOWNERS reconciliation, no review-rule reconciliation, no escalation-policy reconciliation, no repository-access review, no stale privileged access review, no ownerless protected-path review, no primary-only path review, no two-person control, no owner coverage claim, no release authority claim, no release promotion claim, no stable signing authority claim, no update trust claim, no supported-version changes claim, no security-disclosure authority claim, no irreversible migration approval claim, no legal approval claim, no incident closure authority claim, no production authority claim, no broad readiness claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template reviews must not name placeholder reviewers, title-only approvers, aliases without owner-reviewed evidence, or private contact details; a real review must replace null fields with retained evidence beyond the checked no-claim backup-ownership readiness-review template.

qualification_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed role level for every named backup owner.
Subsystem competence evidence for every named backup owner.
Representative protected paths covered by each backup owner.
Recent review record or accepted equivalent for every backup owner.
Availability windows and escalation limits for every backup owner.
Succession plan if a primary or backup owner becomes unavailable.
Recusal rules for conflicts, self-review, embargo, or employment change.
Removal path for stale, compromised, unavailable, or unqualified ownership.
Emergency replacement path that keeps release, signing, disclosure, and incident closure unavailable until reviewed.

reconciliation_axes.required_evidence: CODEOWNERS routing matches named qualified backups and protected paths.
Professional review rules name the correct reviewer scopes and independent-review requirements.
Escalation policy reaches qualified humans without granting agents owner authority.
Support ownership is reconciled without implying supported security versions.
Signing separation remains owner-reviewed and does not follow from backup readiness alone.
Security-disclosure authority stays separate from backup-owner readiness.
Package and release ownership paths remain separate from readiness evidence.
CI and branch protection rules route reviews to qualified maintainers.
Service ownership and on-call paths are reconciled where the scope owns services.
Repository access grants are least-privilege and reviewed.
Stale privileged access is reviewed and removed before owner coverage is claimed.
Protected paths have primary and backup routing or remain explicitly blocked.
Primary-only paths are remediated or explicitly kept blocked.
Blocked status is preserved for every scope lacking a named qualified backup.
Single-owner residual risk is owner-reviewed and accepted only through owner-only controls.

two_person_control_axes.required_evidence: Two-person control remains required for stable signing.
Two-person control remains required for update trust changes.
Two-person control remains required for supported-version changes.
Two-person control remains required for security-disclosure decisions.
Two-person control remains required for irreversible profile migration approval.
Two-person control remains required for release promotion.
Two-person control remains required for legal approval.
Two-person control remains required for incident closure.

authority_boundary_axes.required_evidence: Owner coverage requires named qualified backups and owner-reviewed evidence beyond the checked no-claim backup-ownership readiness-review template.
Release authority requires separate human approval and cannot be inferred from this template.
Release promotion requires separate human release authority.
Stable signing authority requires separate key ceremony and two-person control evidence.
Update trust changes require separate release/security approval.
Supported-version changes require separate support and security approval.
Security-disclosure authority requires separate private-process approval.
Irreversible profile migration approval requires data-loss and release review.
Legal approval requires named legal authority beyond this template.
Incident closure requires named incident authority beyond this template.
Production authority requires production-readiness approval beyond this template.
Broad readiness and implementation claims require closed PB gates and owner approval beyond this template.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Named owner reviewer accepts or keeps blocked every critical scope beyond the checked no-claim backup-ownership readiness-review template.
Named independent reviewer verifies qualification, reconciliation, and two-person-control evidence.
Security reviewer confirms disclosure, incident, stale-access, and privileged-access boundaries.
Release-operations reviewer confirms signing, package, update, and release-promotion boundaries.
Legal reviewer confirms legal-community and legal-approval boundaries where applicable.
Support reviewer confirms support and supported-version boundaries where applicable.
Review retains source records without private contact details or unsupported authority claims.
Any missing evidence keeps PB-019 blocked and keeps authority unavailable.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim template is cited as backup ownership readiness or owner coverage.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the claim and keep PB-019 blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: A backup owner is a placeholder, title-only label, or null in a non-template record.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner coverage and keep PB-019 blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: Candidate evidence is only self-nomination without independent owner review.
Owner identity verification is missing for any named backup.
Role level evidence is missing for any scope.
Subsystem competence evidence is missing for any scope.
Recent review record is missing for a named backup.

rejection_rules.outcome: Keep escalation and owner coverage blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: Succession or recusal evidence is missing.
Inactivity, removal, or emergency replacement evidence is missing.
Review-rule or escalation-policy reconciliation is missing.
Repository access or stale privileged access is unreviewed.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject production authority and owner coverage.

rejection_rules.condition: Ownerless protected-path or primary-only path remains unreviewed.
Two-person control evidence is missing for release or incident authority paths.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, and incident closure claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Backup readiness is used to claim release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, incident closure, production authority, broad readiness, or implementation approval.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the claim and keep owner-only gates blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: A review narrows the claim boundary without updating PB-019, TASK-000008, crosswalk, repository map, and validation.
